english: its flesh is well-tasted, and its hair is supposed to cure cases of poisoning(!) Med. Hook., (Him. Journ. II, 132) seems to mean this animal by his 'gnow', prob. confounding gna (gna') with gnyan (gnyan) (q.v.) which latter, acc. to Cunningham's Ladak p. 198, and by the statements of the natives, is the argali.

ancient Cs., gna-sṅón (gna' sngon) formerly, in old times Cs.; gná-dus (gna' dus) Lex. former times, time of yore; gná-nas ma mtoṅ (gna' nas ma mthong) never seen or heard of before Dzl.; gná-rabs (gna' rabs) Cs. men who lived in old times, the ancients.

1. place, spot, B., C. (in W. sa(-kyád) (sa khyad), sa-čá (sa cha)) dbén-pai gnas šig (dben pa'i gnas shig) a lonely place; mtó-bai gnas (mtho ba'i gnas) a raised place, an elevation Dzl.; gnás-na ⸰dug-pa (gnas na 'dug pa), gnás-su sdód-pa (gnas su sdod pa) the being somewhere, gnás-su ⸰gró-ba (gnas su 'gro ba) the going somewhere, gnás-nas skrod-pa (gnas nas skrod pa) the expelling from a place Gram. - 2 place of residence, abode, dwelling-place, (in W. not in use) gnas ⸰bébspa (gnas 'bebs pa) Sch., ⸰čá-ba ('cha' ba) Ma., ⸰débs-pa ('debs pa), to establish one's self at a place, to settle, gnas gyón-ba (gnas gyon ba), šóm-pa (shom pa), to quarter, lodge, take in, a person Stg., gnas méd-par ⸰gyúr-ba (gnas med par 'gyur ba) to become homeless; a house, family, or race no longer existing, extinct, Dzl.; gnás-su sóṅ-ṅo (gnas su song ngo) they returned to their place, their home Dzl.; gnas daṅ skyabs méd-par ⸰gyúr-ba (gnas dang skyabs med par 'gyur ba) to be at one's wit's end, not knowing what to do Schr. - 3. a holy place, place of pilgrimage; hermitage, monastery; *nás j̇al-pa (gnas mjal pa), nás-kor-pa* (gnas skor pa) W. a pilgrim; *dor-je-liṅ-gi ne* (rdo rje gling gi gnas) the hermitage, or Buddhist parsonage in Darjeeling; acc. to Sch. also Lama, cf. mčód-gnas (mchod gnas). - 4. a clerical dignity or degree, gnas sbyín-pa (gnas sbyin pa) to confer such Sch. - 5. (cf. the Latin locus) object, like yul (yul), but not so frq., gád-moi gnas (gad mo'i gnas) an object of laughter; ṅó-tṣai gnas (ngo tsha'i gnas) words, actions, which ought to be an object of shame Schr.; point, head, item Was. (225); sphere, province, fig. S.g.; rig-pai gnas lṅa (rig pa'i gnas lnga) the five classes of science. - gnas ⸰gyúr-ba (gnas 'gyur ba) Sch.: to appear embodied (?); gnás-su ⸰gyúr-ba (gnas su 'gyur ba) and byéd-pa (byed pa) S.O. and elsewh.?

(imp. prob. only in the periphrastical form gnás-par byos (gnas par byos)) 1. to be, live, lodge, dwell, stay, of persons, animals and things, mṅál-na gnás-pai kyeu (mngal na gnas pa'i khye'u) the babes in their mother's womb Dom. - 2. to remain, hold to or on, adhere to, e.g. a doctrine, opinion, way of acting etc., dgé-ba bcú-la gnás-pa (dge ba bcu la gnas pa) to persevere in the ten virtues; byáms-pai séms-la gnás-pa (byams pa'i sems la gnas pa) to remain, to continue in love; in a general sense: čós-la gnás-pa (chos la gnas pa) 'one abiding in religion', a clerical person Dzl. 2, 13; to exist permanently, opp. to the moment of first taking existence Was. (278). - 3. to hesitate (?). - ráb-tu gnás-pa (rab tu gnas pa) v. ráb-tu (rab tu). Comp. and deriv. (also of gnas): gnás-skabs (gnas skabs) 1. state, condition, or perh. more accurately period, mṅál-gyi gnás-skabs ltár-ltar-po (mngal gyi gnas skabs ltar ltar po) Lex. 2. temporal life, gnás-skabs-kyi bdé-ba (gnas skabs kyi bde ba) temporal happiness (opp. to mtár-tug-gi (mthar thug gi) snyíṅ-po (snying po), or don (don), ⸰brás-bu ('bras bu), Schr., the essence or result of perfection, here, therefore, = eternal felicity); gnás-skabs-tse-yi bar-gcód mi ⸰byúṅ-žiṅ (gnas skabs tshe yi bar gcod mi 'byung zhing) if my temporal life be not endangered. - gnás-kaṅ (gnas khang) dwelling, dwelling-house or room Dzl.; gnás-kaṅ-la sógs-pa (gnas khang la sogs pa) a furnished house or room Dzl. - gnas-čén (gnas chen) a great resort of pilgrimage, a great sanctuary Tar. - gnas brtán (gnas brtan) (loco firmus, stabilis, lit. translation of स्थविर 1. firm, 2. old) an elder, senior, n. of the (16) highest disciples of Buddha; afterwards, when various schools had been formed, n. of the orthodox Buddhists, Burn. I, 288; Köpp. I, 383; Was. (38). (Cs. seems to have confounded brtan (brtan) with brten (brten), when he translates: subaltern, vicar). - gnás-po (gnas po) host, landlord, master of a house, head of a family C., gnás-mo (gnas mo) fem. Glr. - gnas-mál (gnas mal) Lex., शय्यासन, sleeping-place, night-quarters, couch Schr.; Cs. dwelling-place (?) - gnas-med (gnas med) v. gnas 2. (gnas). - gnas gtsáṅ-mai ris (gnas gtsang ma'i ris) n. p., name of an abode of the gods. - gnas-tsáṅ (gnas tshang) dwelling, quarters, lodgings, mi-la gnas-tsáṅ gyár-ba (mi la gnas tshang g.yar ba) to ask for a lodging; to be <
